---
title: "Benthic Habitat Mapping"
description: "GIS-based mapping and classification of benthic habitats using remote sensing and field survey data."
date: "2025-03-01"
categories: [GIS, marine science, mapping, remote sensing]
image: ../images/benthic-map.jpg
---

## Overview

Benthic habitat maps are essential tools for coral reef management and 
conservation. This project explores methods for producing high-resolution 
habitat maps by combining field survey data with remote sensing imagery.

## Methods

### Data Sources
- Multispectral satellite imagery
- Underwater photo transects
- SfM-derived orthomosaics

### Classification Approach
- Object-based image analysis (OBIA)
- Supervised classification using field validation points
- Accuracy assessment with confusion matrices

### GIS Processing
- Coordinate reference system management
- Raster and vector integration
- Map layout and cartographic output

## Tools Used

- QGIS
- Python (rasterio, geopandas, scikit-learn)
- Google Earth Engine (planned)

## Status

🚧 *In progress — methods being developed.*
